question: Based on Table 1.3-1 comparison, Table 5.2-4 materials, and Figure 5.4-1 SG design, does the NuScale SG offer safety advantages over conventional PWR steam generators?

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in each NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer and safety valves. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, thermal relief valves, and access ports, with the RPV itself forming the outer pressure boundary for the SGs and contributing to the reactor coolant pressure boundary through its tubes and welds.

nuscale_ch05_0013.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0013.summary: This section lists references and standards used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report regarding the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary. It includes guidelines and reports from the Electric Power Research Institute and the American Society of Mechanical Engineers, as well as tables summarizing relevant ASME code cases for reactor components.

nuscale_ch01_0558.title: DSRS 12.3-12.4, Rev 0: Radiation Protection Design FeaturesII.9 RG 8.15
nuscale_ch01_0558.summary: This section summarizes the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report’s conformance with various regulatory guides related to radiation protection design features. It details whether each Regulatory Guide (RG) is applicable, not applicable, or partially/conforms, with references to supporting sections or tables for further information. Specifically, RG 8.15 is noted as not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0052.title: Comparison with Other Facilities
nuscale_ch01_0052.summary: This section compares the major design features and nominal parameters of the NuScale Power Plant with those of a typical pressurized water reactor (PWR). It highlights differences in aspects such as electrical output, core design, safety systems, and reactor components, using summary tables for direct comparison. The data provided are nominal values intended for reference only.

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency cooling valves, and associated piping. It details how the steam generators are integrated into the reactor vessel, forming part of the primary and secondary pressure boundaries, and references other sections for further information on specific components.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with NRC regulations and ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) requirements for overpressure protection of the reactor coolant boundary. It details how code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing are selected and verified according to specific regulatory guides and federal regulations, ensuring all conditions and requirements are met. Table 5.2-1 consolidates the specific ASME code cases used.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the NuScale pressurizer and its components, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It references relevant sections of the BPVC (Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code) and lists the specific alloys and material specifications for the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, bolting, and heater sheaths.

nuscale_ch01_0450.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsTFSGD B.1 thru B.4Top-Feed Steam Generator Designs
nuscale_ch01_0450.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use top-feed, preheat, or once-through steam generator designs, so the specific regulatory guidelines for avoiding water hammers in these systems are not applicable. Instead, NuScale uses a passive natural circulation system (DHRS) that fulfills a similar safety function without the need for auxiliary feedwater pumps or external supply headers, thereby inherently avoiding the water hammer issues addressed by these guidelines.

nuscale_ch01_0112.title: Figure 1.7-3a: Piping and Instrumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0112.summary: This section provides the legend for Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report, detailing the standardized symbols and notations for various valves, vents, drains, fire safety equipment, and specialty items. It specifies that all vents and drains are represented as 0.75" normally closed valves unless otherwise noted, and includes notes on how to interpret flow paths and system abbreviations.

nuscale_ch01_0648.title: DSRS 15.0, Rev 0: Introduction - Transient and Accident AnalysesI.5 Plant Characteristics Considered in the Safety Evaluation
nuscale_ch01_0648.summary: Section I.5, "Plant Characteristics Considered in the Safety Evaluation," confirms that the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report fully conforms to the regulatory criteria outlined in DSRS 15.0, Rev 0. No exceptions or deviations are noted for this section.

nuscale_ch01_0650.title: DSRS 15.0, Rev 0: Introduction - Transient and Accident AnalysesI.7 Evaluation of Individual Initiating Events
nuscale_ch01_0650.summary: This section summarizes NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria for transient and accident analyses, specifically regarding the evaluation of individual initiating events. It states that the evaluation of individual initiating events fully conforms to the applicable regulatory guidance, with no exceptions noted.

nuscale_ch01_0060.title: NuScale Integral System Test Program
nuscale_ch01_0060.summary: The NuScale Integral System Test Program was established to collect thermal-hydraulic data for system characterization and to validate safety analysis codes using a scaled model of the NuScale Power Module (NPM) design. The NIST-1 facility replicates key reactor systems and supports the development and validation of proprietary safety codes, ensuring accurate modeling of the NPM’s unique features. Testing at NIST-1 has provided essential data for NuScale’s safety analysis and design certification efforts.

nuscale_ch01_0772.title: SRP 15.6.3, Rev 2: Radiological Consequences of Steam Generator Tube Failure (PWR)II (No Number) First paragraph and Items (1) and (2) of Section II on page 15.6.32 - Acceptability of Site and Dose Mitigating ESF Systems
nuscale_ch01_0772.summary: The section explains that the guidance for calculating radiological consequences of a steam generator tube failure is applicable to the NuScale Design Certification Application (DCA). However, the specific radiological acceptance criteria referenced in this guidance are superseded by those in SRP Section 15.0.3, meaning only the calculation methodology is followed, not the acceptance limits. This results in only partial conformance with the original guidance.

nuscale_ch01_0188.title: BTP 6-1, (March 2007): pH for Emergency Coolant Water for Pressurized Water ReactorsB.3 Hydrogen Generation from Aluminum Corrosion
nuscale_ch01_0188.summary: The section reviews NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria regarding pH control and hydrogen generation from aluminum corrosion in emergency coolant water. While NuScale's design lacks certain systems referenced in the criteria (such as containment spray systems and sumps), the intent of the guidelines is still met due to the collection and recirculation of ECCS water within the containment vessel. Other criteria, such as those related to minimum containment pressure and dual containment leakage, are deemed not applicable to the NuScale design.

nuscale_ch01_0446.title: DSRS 10.4.7, Rev 0: Condensate and Feedwater SystemII.7 Flow Accelerated Corrosion
nuscale_ch01_0446.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria for the Condensate and Feedwater System, specifically regarding flow accelerated corrosion, stating that the design fully conforms to requirements in this area. It also notes that certain criteria related to heat removal, inspection, and testing are not applicable to the NuScale design, and that systems such as steam generator blowdown and auxiliary feedwater are not used in this design.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system piping, specifically focusing on steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the main degradation observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that the NuScale design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not introduce new risks, as validated by testing. The assessment also identifies potential secondary side corrosion mechanisms, but estimates their impact to be low, supporting the use of standard SG tube plugging criteria.

nuscale_ch01_0022.title: Engineered Safety Feature Materials
nuscale_ch01_0022.summary: The NuScale reactor’s containment vessel (CNV) is maintained under vacuum during normal operation, which reduces corrosion, aids in leak detection, limits oxygen for combustible gas control, and eliminates the need for insulation. The design relies on passive safety features—such as passive cooling and emergency core cooling—which, along with a smaller fuel inventory and elimination of external coolant loops, significantly enhance safety and reduce core damage frequency compared to conventional reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0059.title: Steam Generator Thermal-Hydraulic Performance Testing - Fluid-Heated Facility
nuscale_ch01_0059.summary: The section describes thermal-hydraulic performance testing of NuScale steam generator tubes using an electrically-heated setup at the SIET facility in Italy. The tests, which included various thermal and dynamic conditions, allowed precise control and measurement of heat flux and pressure fluctuations, supporting design validation, code benchmarking, and regulatory inspection for safe and stable steam generator operation.

nuscale_ch01_0003.title: Plant Location
nuscale_ch01_0003.summary: The section introduces the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report (FSAR), which is required for standard design certification under 10 CFR 52. It describes the modular and scalable design of the NuScale Power Module (NPM), highlighting key safety features such as passive shutdown and cooling, compact containment, and advanced digital controls, with the plant capable of operating up to twelve modules within a single facility.

nuscale_ch01_0020.title: Safety Considerations
nuscale_ch01_0020.summary: The NuScale design enhances safety by maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel (CNV), which reduces corrosion, aids in leak detection, and limits combustible gases. Its passive safety systems and integral design eliminate large-break loss-of-coolant accident (LOCA) scenarios and reduce reliance on external power, resulting in a lower core damage frequency compared to conventional light water reactors.

nuscale_ch01_0113.title: Figure 1.7-3b: Piping and In strumentation Diagram Legends
nuscale_ch01_0113.summary: This section provides the legend for Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs) used in the NuScale Final Safety Analysis Report. It explains the standard symbols and abbreviations for valve positions, actuator types, and other instrumentation details, clarifying how to interpret valve status (e.g., normally open/closed, fail positions) and actuator mechanisms on the diagrams.

edges.description: Section A describes the design and components of the NuScale steam generators, while Section B lists references including material reliability guidelines (such as EPRI and ASME standards) that underpin the material choices and integrity claims made in Section A.

Section A describes the design features of the NuScale steam generators, while Section B outlines the regulatory criteria for radiation protection that the design must meet, connecting them through the requirement that the SG design must conform to or partially conform with these radiation protection standards.

Section A describes the specific design features and components of the NuScale steam generators, while Section B provides a comparative table (Table 1.3-1) that contrasts these features and parameters with those of conventional PWR steam generators, thus connecting the detailed design information in A to the comparative safety evaluation in B.

Section A describes the integrated design of the NuScale Power Module, including the steam generators and their safety-related components, while Section B outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that the reactor coolant boundary—including the steam generators—must comply with, connecting the design features in A to the regulatory requirements in B.

Section A describes the overall design and components of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including the steam generators, while Section B references where the materials used in the pressurizer (and by extension, related RPV components) are specified, thus connecting the general system design in A to the detailed material specifications in B.

Section A describes the integrated design of the NuScale steam generators within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B references regulatory guidelines for steam generator design and clarifies that certain guidelines for large PWRs do not apply to NuScale due to its different DHRS configuration, connecting them through the applicability and conformance of design features to regulatory expectations.

Section A lists references that provide technical standards and material reliability data relevant to the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary, which are foundational for demonstrating conformance with the radiation protection and regulatory criteria outlined in Section B.

Section A provides references to industry standards and materials reliability programs that underpin the material choices and quality assurance for the NuScale SG, while Section B presents a comparative table (Table 1.3-1) that uses these material and design choices to contrast NuScale's safety features with those of conventional PWR steam generators.

Section A provides references to industry standards and materials reliability programs that are directly relevant to the materials and quality assurance aspects described in Section B's discussion of the NuScale reactor coolant system and steam generator design.

Section A references industry standards and guidelines (such as ASME and EPRI) that are also cited in Section B as the basis for compliance with regulatory requirements for overpressure protection and material selection, establishing that the design and material choices in Section B are supported by the authoritative sources listed in Section A.

Section B references Section A for material descriptions of the RPV upper shell and upper head, indicating that the material information in Section A is used to specify the components discussed in Section B.

Section A lists references that include materials reliability and quality assurance standards relevant to the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary, while Section B discusses regulatory design guidelines for steam generator systems and notes the applicability (or non-applicability) of certain guidelines to the NuScale design, connecting both sections through their focus on compliance with safety and reliability standards for steam generator design.

Section B provides a direct comparison of NuScale SG design features and parameters (Table 1.3-1) with those of conventional PWRs, which serves as evidence to support the regulatory conformance claims and safety advantages discussed in Section A.

Section A outlines the regulatory criteria and conformance for radiation protection design features, while Section B describes the physical design and components of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including the steam generators, thus Section B provides the technical design details that must satisfy the regulatory requirements specified in Section A.

Section A outlines the NuScale SG's conformance with radiation protection regulatory criteria, while Section B details compliance with ASME and NRC overpressure protection requirements, both of which are regulatory prerequisites that must be satisfied to demonstrate the SG's overall safety advantages compared to conventional PWRs.

Section A outlines the regulatory criteria and conformance for radiation protection design features, while Section B provides specific material details for reactor coolant system components, with the material information in Section B serving as evidence to support claims of regulatory conformance discussed in Section A.

Section A outlines NuScale's conformance with radiation protection regulatory criteria, while Section B discusses design guidelines for steam generator systems and clarifies that certain conventional PWR requirements (like AFW system guidelines) are not applicable to NuScale due to its different design (DHRS), thus connecting them through the comparison of regulatory applicability and design features relevant to safety advantages.

Section A provides a comparative overview of NuScale's design features and parameters (including steam generator aspects) relative to conventional PWRs, while Section B details the specific design and configuration of the NuScale reactor coolant system and its steam generators, thus Section B provides the technical basis that underpins the comparative claims made in Section A.

Section A provides a comparative overview of NuScale's design features and parameters relative to conventional PWRs, while Section B details the compliance of NuScale's reactor coolant boundary (including the SG) with ASME and NRC regulatory requirements, connecting the comparison in A to the regulatory and material standards in B.

Section A provides a comparative overview of NuScale and conventional PWR design features (including steam generator parameters), while Section B details the specific materials used in NuScale's reactor coolant system components, which are referenced in the comparative analysis.

Section A provides a comparative overview of NuScale's steam generator design features and parameters relative to conventional PWRs, while Section B discusses regulatory design guidelines for steam generator systems and clarifies that certain guidelines for large PWRs do not apply to NuScale due to its different design (DHRS instead of AFW), thus connecting them through the context of design differences and regulatory applicability.

Section A describes the integrated design of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including the steam generators, while Section B outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that the design must comply with, connecting the physical design features in A to the regulatory requirements in B.

Section A provides a general overview of the integrated design of the NuScale Power Module, including the steam generators, while Section B references specific material details for pressurizer components, linking back to material specifications in other sections.

Section A describes the integrated design of the NuScale reactor coolant system, including its steam generators, while Section B outlines regulatory guidelines (BTP 10-2) for avoiding water hammers in steam generators and notes how NuScale's design conforms to or differs from these guidelines.
Section B references Section A by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions relevant to the pressurizer, establishing a cross-reference for detailed information.

Section A describes compliance with ASME and NRC overpressure protection codes for the reactor coolant boundary, while Section B discusses conformance with regulatory guidelines for steam generator design, specifically noting that certain guidelines for auxiliary feedwater systems do not apply to NuScale due to its different design; thus, Section A's code compliance is a prerequisite for evaluating the applicability and sufficiency of the design guidelines discussed in Section B.

Section A references Section B by noting that material specifications and component descriptions in Section A are further detailed or cross-referenced in Section B, particularly regarding regulatory guidelines and conformance for steam generator and pressurizer components.

Section A provides the legend and symbology for interpreting piping and instrumentation diagrams, which is necessary for understanding the detailed design and flow paths described in Section B's discussion of the NuScale steam generator and reactor coolant system components.

Section A provides diagram legends for interpreting P&IDs, which may be necessary to understand the system configurations referenced in Section B's cited standards and guidelines for reactor coolant boundary integrity.

Section A provides diagram legends for interpreting P&IDs, which are necessary for understanding the system configurations relevant to the radiation protection design features and regulatory conformance discussed in Section B.

Section A outlines the regulatory criteria and safety evaluation framework that the NuScale SG design in Section B must conform to, establishing the standards against which the SG's safety advantages are assessed.

Section A outlines the regulatory criteria and safety evaluation framework that the NuScale FSAR must conform to, while Section B lists the technical references (such as EPRI chemistry guidelines and ASME QA standards) that provide the material and operational standards supporting the integrity claims for the reactor coolant boundary, thus connecting the regulatory requirements in A with the technical evidence in B.

Section A outlines the plant characteristics and safety analysis approach considered in the NuScale FSAR, while Section B details the radiation protection design features and their conformance with regulatory guidance, both providing regulatory context for evaluating whether the NuScale SG design offers safety advantages over conventional PWR SGs.

Section A outlines the regulatory criteria and conformance requirements for transient and accident analyses, which set the safety performance expectations that the steam generator design details in Section B must satisfy.

Section A discusses the NuScale FSAR's conformance with regulatory criteria for transient and accident analyses, while Section B lists references (such as EPRI and ASME standards) that provide the technical basis for evaluating the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary, which is relevant to assessing the safety advantages of the NuScale SG as required in Section A.

Section A discusses the NuScale FSAR's conformance with regulatory criteria for transient and accident analyses, while Section B addresses conformance with radiation protection design features; both sections are connected through their evaluation of how the NuScale design aligns with specific regulatory requirements, but they focus on different safety aspects (accident analysis vs. radiation protection).

Section A describes the integral system test program that generates thermal-hydraulic data and validates safety methodologies, which provides supporting evidence for the safety claims and design features of the steam generators detailed in Section B.

Section A describes the NuScale integral system test program, which generates data and validates safety methodologies relevant to the reactor coolant system, while Section B lists references, including industry guidelines and standards, that inform the integrity and material reliability requirements for the reactor coolant boundary—thus, Section A's test data supports the claims and requirements referenced in Section B.

Section A describes the NuScale integral system test program that generates data to validate safety methodologies, which supports Section B's discussion of conformance with radiation protection regulatory criteria by providing evidence that the design's safety features are validated.

Section A provides diagram legends for interpreting P&IDs, which are necessary for understanding system configurations referenced in Section B's safety evaluation of plant characteristics during transient and accident analyses.
Section A provides diagram legends for interpreting P&IDs, which are necessary for understanding system configurations referenced in Section B's discussion of transient and accident analyses.
Section A provides diagram legends for interpreting P&IDs, which are necessary for understanding the system configurations tested and described in Section B's integral system test program.

Section A provides the general plant characteristics and regulatory conformance context that frames the safety evaluation, while Section B details the evaluation of individual initiating events, which relies on the plant characteristics described in Section A.

Section B provides experimental thermal-hydraulic data and validation that supports the safety evaluation framework described in Section A by supplying evidence relevant to the plant characteristics considered in regulatory safety analyses.

Section B's integral system test program provides thermal-hydraulic data and validation evidence that supports the safety analysis methodologies referenced in Section A for evaluating transient and accident scenarios in the NuScale design.

Section A outlines the regulatory requirements and acceptance criteria for radiological consequences of steam generator tube failure, while Section B describes the design features of the NuScale steam generators that are intended to meet or exceed these regulatory requirements.

Section A discusses the regulatory requirements and acceptance criteria for radiological consequences and site acceptability in the event of steam generator tube failure, while Section B lists the materials standards and references (such as EPRI and ASME guidelines) that inform the selection and qualification of materials used in the NuScale steam generator, thereby connecting the regulatory expectations in A with the technical implementation in B.

Section A discusses the acceptability of site and dose mitigating engineered safety features (ESF) systems in the context of radiological consequences from steam generator tube failures, while Section B outlines the radiation protection design features and their conformance to regulatory guidance, thus both sections are related through their focus on regulatory compliance for radiological safety and mitigation features in the NuScale SG design.

Section A discusses regulatory requirements for water chemistry and pH control relevant to hydrogen generation and fission product removal, noting partial applicability to NuScale due to design differences, while Section B describes the integrated design of the NuScale reactor module, including the steam generators, which are affected by and must conform to the water chemistry requirements referenced in Section A.

Section A discusses NuScale's partial conformance with BTP 6-1 water chemistry requirements, while Section B lists references, including EPRI guidelines on primary water chemistry, that inform or support the technical basis for NuScale's material and chemistry choices.

Section A discusses NuScale's partial conformance with chemical and water chemistry requirements (such as pH control) relevant to safety functions, while Section B addresses NuScale's partial conformance with radiation protection design features, both referencing how the NuScale design meets or partially meets different regulatory criteria for safety, thus both sections provide evidence that supports the overall safety evaluation of the NuScale SG compared to conventional PWRs.

Section A discusses regulatory criteria and partial conformance regarding protection from fluid instabilities in condensate and feedwater systems, while Section B describes the integrated design of the NuScale reactor pressure vessel, including the steam generators, thus Section B provides the specific design context that Section A's regulatory discussion applies to.

Section A references regulatory criteria for flow-accelerated corrosion and fluid instabilities in the NuScale design, while Section B lists industry standards and guidelines (such as EPRI and ASME) that provide technical bases for material selection and corrosion resistance, thus Section A's regulatory conformance is supported by the technical standards cited in Section B.

Section A discusses NuScale's partial conformance with regulatory criteria for protecting systems from fluid instabilities (such as flow accelerated corrosion), while Section B addresses partial conformance with radiation protection regulatory guides, both referencing the NuScale FSAR's approach to meeting safety-related requirements, thus connecting through their evaluation of NuScale's compliance with different but complementary safety criteria.

Section A discusses specific degradation mechanisms and risk comparisons for SG tubing materials, while Section B provides a general description of the integrated NuScale Power Module design, including the inclusion of steam generators, thus Section A provides details/specifications for the general system described in Section B.

Section A discusses observed degradation mechanisms in SG tubing and references industry guidelines, while Section B lists those specific guidelines (e.g., EPRI chemistry and materials reliability programs) that inform the safety analysis in Section A.

Section A discusses the NuScale SG tube design and its degradation mechanisms compared to conventional PWRs, while Section B lists the regulatory conformance status for radiation protection features, connecting them through the evaluation of whether the SG design meets or partially meets relevant safety and radiation protection requirements.

Section A discusses the acceptability of site and dose mitigating engineered safety features (ESF) systems in the context of radiological consequences from steam generator tube failure, while Section B addresses water chemistry requirements (specifically pH control) for emergency coolant water, both of which are connected through their impact on mitigating radiological releases and ensuring safety during postulated accident scenarios.

Section A discusses regulatory criteria for evaluating radiological consequences of steam generator tube failures, while Section B addresses regulatory criteria for protecting systems from flow instabilities such as flow accelerated corrosion, both of which are relevant to assessing the safety performance and advantages of the NuScale steam generator design compared to conventional PWRs.

Section B provides specific design details and operational experience of NuScale SG tubing materials and degradation mechanisms, which serve as evidence to support the regulatory conformance claims and safety evaluations discussed in Section A regarding radiological consequences and acceptability of safety systems.

Both Section A and Section B discuss how NuScale's design partially conforms to regulatory criteria originally written for conventional PWRs, highlighting that specific requirements (such as containment spray systems or large-LWR-specific fluid instability guidance) are not directly applicable due to differences in NuScale's system design and materials.

Section A discusses the partial applicability of regulatory criteria (BTP 6-1) to the NuScale design due to differences in system components, while Section B compares the NuScale SG tubing material and degradation mechanisms to those in conventional PWRs, connecting them through the evaluation of design differences and regulatory conformance.

Section A discusses partial conformance to regulatory criteria for protecting SSCs from fluid flow instability, while Section B provides evidence that the NuScale SG tubing experiences only wear similar to conventional PWRs, supporting the claim that the NuScale SG design does not introduce new degradation mechanisms and thus aligns with the safety intent of the criteria in Section A.

Section A describes how maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel minimizes moisture and corrosion, which supports the reliability and longevity of components such as the steam generators discussed in Section B.

Section A discusses the benefits of maintaining a vacuum in the CNV to minimize corrosion and improve reliability, while Section B lists references including EPRI guidelines and ASME standards that provide the technical basis for material selection and corrosion resistance, connecting A's claims to established industry practices.

Section A describes engineered safety feature materials and design choices (such as maintaining a vacuum in the CNV) that contribute to minimizing corrosion and facilitating leak detection, while Section B lists regulatory criteria and the degree of NuScale's conformance to radiation protection requirements, connecting them through the safety performance and regulatory compliance of the SG design.

Section A describes the thermal-hydraulic performance testing methodology for the NuScale steam generator tubes, which provides empirical data supporting the design and operational claims made in Section B regarding the integrated steam generator's configuration and safety features within the reactor coolant system.

Section A describes the thermal-hydraulic performance testing methodology for the NuScale steam generator, while Section B lists references including industry guidelines and material reliability programs that provide the standards and background for evaluating the integrity and performance of steam generator materials, thus Section A's testing approach is informed by or evaluated against the standards referenced in Section B.

Section A describes the thermal-hydraulic performance testing of the NuScale steam generator, while Section B lists the regulatory criteria and conformance related to radiation protection design features, connecting them through the need for tested performance data (A) to demonstrate compliance with safety-related regulatory requirements (B).

Section A provides a general introduction and regulatory context for the NuScale plant, while Section B details the specific design and components of the reactor coolant system, including the steam generators, thus Section A sets the framework within which Section B's technical details are evaluated.

Section A provides a general introduction to the NuScale FSAR and its regulatory context, while Section B lists references that support the technical and regulatory basis for the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary, including materials and quality assurance standards relevant to the analysis question.

Section A provides a general introduction and scope of the NuScale FSAR, while Section B details the conformance of the NuScale design with specific radiation protection regulatory criteria, with both sections connected by their reference to the overall safety analysis and regulatory compliance of the NuScale plant.

Section A outlines safety benefits of maintaining a vacuum in the containment vessel, which indirectly supports the reliability and corrosion resistance of components such as the steam generators described in Section B, thereby providing evidence that supports the safety claims related to the NuScale SG design.

Section A discusses safety benefits related to corrosion and leakage detection in the NuScale containment, while Section B cites industry guidelines and materials reliability programs that provide the technical basis for evaluating such safety considerations, connecting them through the topic of material integrity and corrosion resistance.

Section A provides legend information for interpreting P&ID diagrams, which is necessary for understanding the system configurations relevant to safety features discussed in Section B's regulatory conformance statements.

Section A describes the use of a vacuum in the containment vessel to minimize corrosion and moisture, which supports the reliability of steam generator components whose thermal-hydraulic performance is tested and characterized in Section B.

Section A describes specific engineered safety features and their material benefits within the containment vessel, while Section B provides a general introduction to the plant and the scope of the safety analysis report, establishing the context in which Section A's details are evaluated.

Section A and Section B are identical text excerpts describing the benefits of maintaining a vacuum in the CNV, including reduced corrosion and improved safety, thus Section A directly supports the safety claims made in Section B.

Section A describes specific thermal-hydraulic performance testing methods for the NuScale steam generator, while Section B provides a general introduction to the plant and its safety analysis report, with Section A's detailed testing supporting the broader safety claims outlined in Section B.

Section A discusses safety benefits of maintaining a vacuum in the CNV, while Section B provides P&ID legend details for valve positions, with both sections relating through operational configurations that impact system safety and reliability.
